title: Density Calculator (Mass / Volume)
shortDescription: Calculate density from mass and volume in g/cm³.
description: Find density instantly using the formula ρ = mass / volume. Enter mass in grams and volume in cm³ to get the result in g/cm³.
metaDescription: Free density calculator: enter mass (g) and volume (cm³) to get density in g/cm³ using ρ = m / V. Fast, accurate, and ideal for physics and chemistry.

result.label: Density
result.empty: Enter mass and volume to calculate density.

sections.heading: What is density?
paragraphs: Density is how much mass is packed into a given volume. It is defined by the formula ρ = mass / volume, where ρ (rho) is density.
When mass is in grams and volume is in cm³, the density comes out in g/cm³, a common unit in physics and chemistry.

sections.heading: Why density matters
paragraphs: Density helps identify materials, predict whether an object floats or sinks, and compare substances. Water, for example, has a density of about 1 g/cm³.
Engineers, scientists, and students use density to analyze materials and verify the purity of samples.

howTo.name: Enter the mass
howTo.text: Type the object's mass in grams (g) into the mass field.

howTo.name: Enter the volume
howTo.text: Type the volume in cubic centimeters (cm³) into the volume field.

howTo.name: Read the density
howTo.text: The calculator divides mass by volume and shows the density in g/cm³.

faq.a: Density equals mass divided by volume: ρ = m / V. With grams and cm³, the result is in g/cm³.

faq.q: What units does this calculator use?
faq.a: Mass is entered in grams (g), volume in cubic centimeters (cm³), and the result is in g/cm³.

faq.q: What is the density of water?
faq.a: Pure water has a density of about 1 g/cm³ at 4 °C, which makes it a useful reference point.

faq.q: How do I convert g/cm³ to kg/m³?
faq.a: Multiply the value in g/cm³ by 1000. For example, 1 g/cm³ equals 1000 kg/m³.
Yes. Materials lighter than water, such as wood or oil, have densities below 1 g/cm³ and float on water.
